data.title: Tamboran Resources launches nearly 3M-share public offering
data.description:
Tamboran Resources (TBN) down 17.1% post-market Tuesday after saying it commenced an underwritten public offering of more than 2.95M common shares, with an underwriters option to purchase up to an additional 15% of common shares.

Tamboran (TBN) said it plans to use the proceeds to fund additional drilling in the Pilot Area, resource delineation in the Orion Acreage and the Beetaloo Central Development Area, drilling in the EP 161 acreage, working capital, and other general corporate purposes.

Last week, the company said it reached a farm-in agreement to advance development of the Beetaloo Basin in Australia's Northern Territory, providing for a staged earn-in up to ~US$28.5M, subject to structured off-ramp provisions.

Tamboran (TBN) agreed to farm down ~10K acres of its working interest across the Shenandoah North Pilot Area and the Shenandoah South Pilot Area—collectively the Pilot Area—and the Beetaloo Central Development Area to Daly Waters Energy.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 3.7M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a larger-than-expected build of 3.7M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending April 3, after increasing by 10.3M barrels in the previous week.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a draw of 4M barrels for the week, distillate inventories reportedly show a draw of 600K barrels, and inventories at the Cushing storage hub reportedly show a draw of 600K barrels.

The Energy Information Administration is scheduled to release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Wednesday; the average of analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecast domestic commercial crude stocks would increase by 600K barrels, gasoline inventories were expected to drop by 1.9M barrels, and distillate inventories were expected to decline by 800K barrels.

WTI front-month April crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $110.50/bbl, down from Tuesday's $112.95/bbl settlement price.

ETF: (USO)

data.title: Large-cap consumer discretionary stocks with A grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season approaches, consumer discretionary stocks are under the microscope as investors assess how American spending held up in the face of persistent inflation pressures, tariff-driven price increases, and shifting consumer confidence.

The following list highlights large-cap consumer discretionary stocks that have earned the highest possible Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A and higher, spanning a wide range of sub-sectors from gaming and automotive retail to leisure products and education services, all indicating that analysts have been consistently raising their earnings expectations ahead of Q1 results.

The list is led by Deckers Outdoor Corporation (DECK) and Tapestry, Inc. (TPR), both earning top A+ ratings for their EPS revision grades. These companies represent leadership positions in the footwear and luxury goods industries, respectively, with market capitalizations of $14.43 billion and $29.09 billion.

The broader list showcases a diverse range of industries within the consumer discretionary sector, from travel and hospitality names like Marriott International, Inc. (MAR) and Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (RCL) to automotive giant General Motors Company (GM). While most stocks on the list carry an A- grade, they all represent high-conviction EPS revision trends that signal positive analyst expectations.

  1. Deckers Outdoor Corporation (DECK) - A+

  2. Tapestry, Inc. (TPR) - A+

  3. Ralph Lauren Corporation (RL) - A

  4. Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) - A-

  5. Burlington Stores, Inc. (BURL) - A-

  6. Chewy, Inc. (CHWY) - A-

  7. Darden Restaurants, Inc. (DRI) - A-

  8. eBay Inc. (EBAY) - A-

  9. Expedia Group, Inc. (EXPE) - A-

  10. Five Below, Inc. (FIVE) - A-

  11. General Motors Company (GM) - A-

  12. Hasbro, Inc. (HAS) - A-

  13. Marriott International, Inc. (MAR) - A-

  14. Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (RCL) - A-

  15. Ross Stores, Inc. (ROST) - A-

  16. SharkNinja, Inc. (SN) - A-

  17. Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (WSM) - A-

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste reports March traffic up 0.6% YoY
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste (ASR) reported ~6.6M passengers in March 2026, up ~0.6% YoY vs. ~6.55M in March 2025.
  • Colombia topped the chart, increasing by ~12.5% YoY to ~1.48M passengers, with a (~12.3%) increase in domestic and a (~13.2%) rise in international traffic.
  • Mexico, the largest market, saw a ~2.4% decline to ~3.81M passengers, with a (~4.0%) drop in international traffic and a (~0.1%) drop in domestic traffic.
  • Puerto Rico traffic fell ~2.3% to ~1.29M, with a (~2.7%) decline in domestic travel, partially offset by a (~1.0%) increase in international traffic.

data.title: Diebold Nixdorf set to join S&P SmallCap 600 index
data.description:
• Diebold Nixdorf (DBD) on Tuesday said it will replace Sealed Air (SEE) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective prior to the opening of trading on Friday, April 10.
  • Clayton, Dubilier & Rice LLC is acquiring Sealed Air in a deal expected to be completed April 9.
  • DBD up 4% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Aehr Test Systems Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.05 beats by $0.02, revenue of $10.31M misses by $0.54M
data.description:
• Aehr Test Systems press release (AEHR): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.05 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $10.31M (-43.7% Y/Y) misses by $0.54M.
  • The company reported quarterly bookings of $37.2 million.
  • Aehr is reiterating its previously provided guidance for the second half of fiscal year 2026, which began November 29, 2025 and ends May 29, 2026, of revenue between $25 million and $30 million and non-GAAP net loss per diluted share between $(0.09) and $(0.05).

data.title: China's central bank buys the most gold in a year as Iran war slashes prices
data.description:
The People's Bank of China said Tuesday it bought the most gold in more than a year in March, as a key pillar of support for the metal remained intact as prices came under pressure due to the Middle East war.

Bullion held by China's central bank rose by 160K oz last month, or around five tons, marking the 17th consecutive month that the PBOC—among the world's largest buyers—has added to its holdings.

Gold tumbled 12% in March in the metal's worst monthly performance since 2008, as the war lifted the U.S. dollar and prompted bets that the Federal Reserve would not reduce interest rates if inflation picked up.

The PBOC's purchases may help lift investor confidence in gold, as some other central banks have turned to sales; in March, Turkey’s central bank sold and swapped ~60 tons to defend the lira.

In the first two months of this year, central banks bought a net 25 tons, the World Gold Council reported last week.

Gold ​futures finished little changed on Tuesday, as traders remained cautious ahead of President Trump's Tuesday night deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or risk devastating attacks on its infrastructure.

Iran showed no sign of accepting Trump's ultimatum, but Pakistan said diplomacy attempting to reach a peaceful settlement is progressing steadily, while asking Trump to extend his deadline by two weeks and Iran to "open the Strait of Hormuz for a corresponding period of two weeks as a goodwill gesture," Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for April delivery added $0.30 to $4,657.10/oz, while front-month Comex April silver (XAGUSD:CUR) fell for the third straight session, down 1.1% to $71.826/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (DUST), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Mid-cap consumer discretionary stocks with A+ grade EPS revisions
data.description:
As Q1 2026 earnings season approaches, consumer discretionary stocks are under the microscope as investors assess how American spending held up in the face of persistent inflation pressures, tariff-driven price increases, and shifting consumer confidence.

The following list highlights mid-cap consumer discretionary stocks that have earned the highest possible Seeking Alpha EPS Revision Quant Grade of A+, spanning a wide range of sub-sectors from gaming and automotive retail to leisure products and education services, all indicating that analysts have been consistently raising their earnings expectations ahead of Q1 results.

The list is topped by American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) and Callaway Golf Company (CALY), both of which have earned the highest EPS revision grade based on quantitative earnings revision analysis. These companies represent diverse segments within the consumer discretionary sector, from apparel retail to leisure products.

Other prominent companies on the list include Columbia Sportswear Company (COLM), known for its outdoor apparel, and Etsy (ETSY), the online marketplace for handmade and vintage goods. 

  1. American Eagle Outfitters, Inc. (AEO) — EPS revision grade: A+

  2. Callaway Golf Company (CALY) — EPS revision grade: A+

  3. Columbia Sportswear Company (COLM) — EPS revision grade: A+

  4. Etsy, Inc. (ETSY) — EPS revision grade: A+

  5. Graham Holdings Company (GHC) — EPS revision grade: A+

  6. Kontoor Brands, Inc. (KTB) — EPS revision grade: A+

  7. LCI Industries (LCII) — EPS revision grade: A+

  8. Under Armour, Inc. (UAA) — EPS revision grade: A+

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: Stocks to watch on Tuesday after market: LEVI, ASTL, GPRO
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages closed mixed on Tuesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ending higher after reports that Pakistan is mediating a two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) closed +0.1%, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) ended +0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) finished -0.2%.

Here are the 3 stocks to watch on the day:

Levi Strauss & Co. (LEVI) rallied 6.0% in postmarket trading to $20.89 after beating estimates with its first-quarter earnings report. The apparel company reported consolidated net revenue up 14% on a reported basis and 9% higher organically, with particular strength in its Beyond Yoga business, which saw organic revenue growth of 23%. Management raised full-year guidance, now expecting EPS in the range of $1.42 to $1.48.

Algoma Steel (ASTL) rallied 5.2% into Tuesday’s close after announcing it formed a joint venture with Canadian defense manufacturer Roshel to establish a center for ballistic steel production. The Roshel Algoma Defence JV will enable sovereign production of ballistic steel solutions to support defense procurements, including light utility vehicles, the Domestic Arctic Mobility Enhancement program, ships, and submarines. 

GoPro (GPRO) announced a restructuring plan that includes a workforce reduction of approximately 145 employees, or roughly 23% of its staff, as the action camera maker works to lower operating costs and improve leverage. The company expects to incur charges of $11.5 million to $15 million related to severance payments and healthcare benefits, with job cuts expected to be substantially completed by year-end.
